scores.correctness_notes: The response invents a detailed library API (TLSCertificatesRequiresV4, CertificateAvailableEvent, CertificateDeniedEvent, renewal_relative_time, renew_certificate, refresh_events, secret_expired handling) that is not documented in the gold standard. The gold standard focuses on the interface contract specification, not Python API details, and notes that specific event names are 'part of the library's Python API, not the interface specification.' The response fabricates a confident, detailed API surface that goes far beyond what the gold standard supports.
scores.hallucination_notes: TLSCertificatesRequiresV4 class name, CertificateAvailableEvent, CertificateDeniedEvent, certificate_denied event, renewal_relative_time parameter, renew_certificate() method, refresh_events parameter, _on_secret_expired/_on_secret_remove handler names, '90% of validity period' renewal timing — all invented details not supported by the gold standard source material
